skill.name: biorxiv-database
skill.description: Search bioRxiv preprints by keyword, author, date range, category, or DOI, then export metadata or download selected PDFs.
skill.summary: Search bioRxiv metadata and download selected preprint PDFs from the public bioRxiv service.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristic combinations, weak cryptography, credential indicators, and many external command hits. Review found no malicious intent, no credential access, no subprocess execution, and no prompt injection; the real risks are intentional public-network requests and user-directed local file writes.

medium_findings.title: Public Network Requests to bioRxiv
medium_findings.description: The Python script opens a requests session and sends HTTPS GET requests to the public bioRxiv API and PDF endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand it contacts external services and may reveal search topics through API requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is semantically confirmed in runtime code and matches the stated purpose. Confidence is high because the destinations are hardcoded public bioRxiv domains, not covert endpoints.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed Local File Writes
medium_findings.description: The CLI writes downloaded PDFs and exported result data to paths supplied by the user. This is legitimate functionality, but it can overwrite files if the caller provides an unsafe output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file writes are present and tied to CLI output options. The risk is bounded because paths are user supplied and no hidden write target was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as External Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command detections occur inside Markdown code fences that document how to run the Python script. They are not executed by the skill itself, but they show that users are expected to run a local CLI script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows documentation examples rather than executable launcher code. Runtime script review found no subprocess, eval, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Credential Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, hardcoded IP, and key-file indicators map to DOI examples, keyword fields, or documentation text. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, certificate files, private keys, or credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts are DOI examples, keyword argument handling, and API response samples. Targeted review did not find secret reads, crypto APIs, or credential exfiltration patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Client Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The script imports requests, creates a session, and uses session.get with timeouts for bioRxiv API and PDF retrieval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in runtime code. It is dangerous only in the general sense that it performs external network access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Output File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes binary PDF content and JSON search results to local paths requested through CLI arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed in runtime code and exposed through documented CLI flags. No hidden or automatic sensitive file writes were found.

content.value_statement: Life sciences researchers need fast access to current preprint metadata. This skill searches bioRxiv, filters results, exports metadata, and downloads selected PDFs.
actual_capabilities: Search bioRxiv records by keyword across titles, abstracts, and author fields.
Find preprints by author name within a selected date range.
Filter searches by supported bioRxiv subject categories.
Retrieve paper details for a specific DOI or DOI URL.
Export structured search results to a local file or standard output.
Download a selected preprint PDF from bioRxiv to a local path.
limitations: The search script only targets bioRxiv, not medRxiv or journal databases.
Keyword and author matching use local substring filters after API retrieval.
Large date ranges can require many API results and slower processing.
PDF download assumes version 1 when downloading directly by DOI.

use_cases.title: Build a Review Seed Set
use_cases.description: Collect recent preprints for a defined topic, category, and date range before manual screening.

use_cases.title: Track Author Activity
use_cases.description: Monitor new bioRxiv papers from collaborators, competitors, or principal investigators over time.

use_cases.title: Prepare Metadata for Analysis
use_cases.description: Export titles, authors, abstracts, DOIs, categories, and dates for downstream trend analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Search a Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search over a recent date range
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for preprints about CRISPR gene editing from the last 90 days and summarize the top themes.

prompt_templates.title: Filter by Category
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused search in one subject area
prompt_templates.prompt: Find bioRxiv papers about machine learning in the bioinformatics category from 2024 and list titles, authors, dates, and DOIs.

prompt_templates.title: Track an Author
prompt_templates.prompt: Search bioRxiv for preprints by Smith since 2023 and identify recurring research topics from the results.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Review Dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a bioRxiv dataset for organoid bioengineering papers from 2023 to 2024, export metadata, and recommend screening criteria.

output_examples.input: Search for CRISPR papers in genomics during 2024.
output_examples.output: A concise result set with matching titles, authors, dates, DOIs, categories, abstracts, and source links.

output_examples.input: Find recent preprints by a named author.
output_examples.output: A filtered author list showing matching preprints, publication dates, DOI identifiers, and brief metadata for review.

output_examples.input: Download the PDF for a specific bioRxiv DOI.
output_examples.output: The selected preprint PDF is saved locally, and the command reports whether the download succeeded.

best_practices: Use narrow date ranges first, then expand searches after checking result quality.
Filter by category when the research area maps clearly to a bioRxiv subject.
Review downloaded PDFs and metadata manually before citing or screening papers.
anti_patterns: Do not treat preprints as peer-reviewed journal articles without verification.
Do not run broad searches repeatedly without respecting API rate limits.
Do not save outputs over important local files when choosing output paths.

faq.question: Which database does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches the public bioRxiv API for life sciences preprints.

faq.question: Can it search by author?
faq.answer: Yes. It retrieves records for a date range and filters author names locally.

faq.question: Can it download full papers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can download selected bioRxiv PDFs when a DOI and output path are provided.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed script uses the public bioRxiv API and does not read credentials.

faq.question: Does it support medRxiv?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed implementation is focused on bioRxiv only.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
